title: Zone Boundary Traffic Monitoring — Anomalous Inter-Zone Volume

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'IEC 62443-3-2 / Zone boundary traffic monitoring' → 'SR 5.1'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
spl:
index=fw sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-14d
| eval zone_pair=concat(src_zone,"->",dest_zone)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats sum(bytes) as b, dc(dest_port) as ports by _time, zone_pair, app
| eventstats median(b) as med by zone_pair, app
| eval ratio=if(med>0, round(b/med,2), null())
| where ratio>8 AND b>10485760
| sort - b
value: Baselines permitted bytes and sessions between OT zones and flags spikes that may indicate policy drift, covert channels, or misrouted traffic at conduits.
implementation: (1) Build `ot_zone_pairs_expected.csv` for pairs that should exist; (2) exclude known maintenance windows; (3) alert on new `app` on sensitive pairs; (4) correlate with change records.
visualization: Time chart (bytes by zone_pair), Heatmap (zone_pair × hour), Table (top spikes).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t dc(All_Traffic.dest_port) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.app span=1h | sort - agg_value

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks

knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
